messages.content: SHORTEN:The underlying mechanisms through which IL-10 influences NK cell function are still being explored. It has been suggested that IL-10 regulates NK cell metabolism and immune via mTORC1 signaling
IL-10's impact on NK cell function is being studied, including its role in regulating NK cell metabolism and immune response through mTORC1 signaling.

SHORTEN:The underlying mechanisms through which IL-10 influences NK cell function are still being explored. It has been suggested that IL-10 regulates NK cell metabolism and immune via mTORC1 signaling
IL-10's effect on NK cell function is under investigation, with a focus on its potential role in regulating NK cell metabolism and immune responses through mTORC1 signaling.

REWRITE:The underlying mechanisms through which IL-10 influences NK cell function are still being explored. It has been suggested that IL-10 regulates NK cell metabolism and immune via mTORC1 signaling
The exact ways in which IL-10 affects NK cell function are still being investigated. One proposed mechanism is that IL-10 regulates NK cell metabolism and immune responses through mTORC1 signaling.
improve:Liver fibrosis is a chronic and progressive disease with excessive accumulation of extracellular matrix components, posing a significant challenge in medicine

Liver fibrosis is a challenging medical condition characterized by the chronic and progressive accumulation of excessive extracellular matrix components, presenting significant obstacles in terms of treatment and management.
improve: Despite existing research has shed light on the role of IL-10 in enhancing NK cell functions
Considerably progressed research has provided valuable insights into the role of IL-10 in enhancing the functions of NK cells.

Give me a good name for this abstract：
Background and Aims: Liver fibrosis is a progressive disease that can lead to cirrhosis or even liver failure. Recent studies have shown that interleukin 10 (IL-10) and natural killer (NK) cells may be effective in fighting against it. However, little is known that whether NK cells play important role in the anti-fibrotic effect of IL-10. This study aimed to investigate the regulatory effects of IL-10 on NK cells in combating liver fibrosis.
Methods: C57BL/6J mice were intraperitoneally injected 10% carbon tetrachloride (CCL4) to induce liver fibrosis model. Fibrotic mice were further treated with or without IL-10 gene in the presence or absence of NK cells by using Asialo GM1 (ASGM1) antibody. Liver damage was assessed by H&E staining and plasma transaminases. Liver fibrosis was visualized by Sirius Red staining and hydroxyproline assay. NK cell distribution in liver tissue was analyzed through immunohistochemistry and immunofluorescence. Flow cytometry was utilized to evaluate the quantity, activation, cytotoxicity, maturity degree, and source of intrahepatic NK cells. Liver chemokines were determined via ELISA.
Results: In the presence of NK cells, IL-10 gene intervention effectively reduced liver injury and collagen deposition, improving liver fibrosis in mice. This intervention enhanced the accumulation of NK cells in fibrotic liver and boosted their function, as evidenced by increased expression of activated receptor NKG2D, as well as the production of anti-inflammatory cytokine IFN-γ and cytotoxic factor CD107a. Furthermore, IL-10 gene intervention regulated the migration of classical NK cells to fibrotic liver tissues, accompanied by an elevation in CCL5 levels. However, depletion of NK cells from the liver exacerbated CCL4-induced liver fibrosis injury and significantly impaired the anti-fibrotic effect of IL-10 gene treatment.
Conclusions: The anti-fibrotic effect of IL-10 is dependent on enhancing the function of natural killer cells in mouse fibrotic liver, by promoting NK cells activation, cytotoxicity, development and migration. These results provide valuable insights into the mechanisms by which IL-10 regulates NK cells to mitigate the progression of liver fibrosis.
Title: "Enhancing Liver Fibrosis Regression: The Impact of IL-10 on Natural Killer Cell Function"

Title: "Unleashing the Power of Natural Killer Cells: IL-10's Impact on Liver Fibrosis Regression"

Title: "Unveiling IL-10's Critical Role in Empowering Natural Killer Cells Against Liver Fibrosis"
